LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALENDER-ROBINSON CO., INC.'S MOTION TO CONTEST THE GOOD FAITH DETERMINATION OF SETTLEMENT [CCP SEC 877.6(A)(2)]: NORTHFIELD INSURANCE CO. AND BASS UNDERWRITERS, INC.'S "APPLICATION FOR GOOD FAITH DETERMINATION (CCP 877.6(A)(2))" IS GRANTED. NORTHFIELD AND BASS'S SETTLEMENT WITH PLAINTIFFS IS WITHIN "THE BALLPARK" OF TECH-BILT, INC. V. WOODWARD-CLYDE ASSOCS. (1985) 38 CAL.3D 488. THIS CASE INVOLVES UNDERINSURANCE REVEALED AFTER A FIRE. APPLICANTS AND PLAINTIFFS ATTRIBUTE THIS PREDOMINATELY TO DEFENDANT CALENDER-ROBINSON CO., INC.'S REPORTS OF INACCURATE BUILDING SQUARE FOOTAGES, AND THUS HAVE AGREED TO AN ARM'S LENGTH SETTLEMENT TOTALING $25,000 (THE POLICY LIMITS ALREADY HAVING BEEN PAID). AS CALENDER-ROBINSON CONCEDES, TECH-BILT REQUIRES ONLY "ROUGH APPROXIMATIONS." ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-24-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NORTHFIELD INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS TOM RICHARDS AND LINDA RICHARDS TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO NORTHFIELD INSURANCE COMPANY'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS [NOS. 1-3, NO. 7, NOS. 10-11, NOS. 13-15 (SET ONE)]; AND TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH AGREEMENT TO PRODUCE [CCP 2031.310, 2031.320], THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS ARE TO PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS 1-3, 7, 10-11, AND 13-15 IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 2031.220-2031.230 AND SHALL PRODUCE DOCUMENTS LABELED IN A MANNER TO IDENTIFY THE REQUEST TO WHICH IT IS RESPONSIVE WITHIN 20-DAYS OF THE ORDER ON THIS MOTION. COURT TO REVIEW SUBMITTED PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint With Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Jun-18-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CALENDER-ROBINSON CO., INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint:

Calendar-Robinson Co., Inc's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is off calendar as moot. Calendar- Robinson seeks to file a cross-complaint for equitable indemnity against Northfield, Bass, and unnamed Roes. Northfield and Bass settled with plaintiff, the court concluded that the settlement is in good faith, and therefore Calendar-Robinson can no longer assert its claims for equitable indemnity against Northfield and Bass. (See CCP 877.6(c).) The motion is therefore moot. If Calendar-Robinson subsequently discovers the names of other co-obligors/tortfeasors, it can seek to plead a cross-complaint against them at that time.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Defendant Calender Robinson Co Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Good Faith Determination Of Settlement [Ccp Sec 877.6(A)(2)]
Off Calendar Jun-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Gerry Mannion, Esq. (415-733-1050; gerry@mannionlowe.com) of Mannion Lowe & Oksenendler for Plaintiffs Tom Richards and Linda Richards.
Lisa L. Kirk, Esq. (415-391-3911; lkirk@ccplaw.com) of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson LLP for Defendant Northfield Insurance Company .
John Levitt, Esq. (805-497-7706; jlevitt@slfesq.com) of Soltman, Levitt, Flaherty & Wattles LLP for Defendant Bass Underwriters, Inc.
Hooshie Broomand, Esq. (916-830-6522; hbroomand@grsm.com) of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani for Defendant Calender-Robinson Co., Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CALENDER-ROBINSON CO., INC.'s Motion To Contest The Good Faith Determination Of Settlement [CCP Sec 877.6(A)(2)]:

Northfield Insurance Co. and Bass Underwriters, Inc.'s "application for good faith determination (CCP 877.6(a)(2))" is granted. Northfield and Bass's settlement with plaintiffs is within "the ballpark" of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde Assocs. (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. This case involves underinsurance revealed after a fire. Applicants and plaintiffs attribute this predominately to defendant Calender-Robinson Co., Inc.'s reports of inaccurate building square footages, and thus have agreed to an arm's length settlement totaling $25,000 (the policy limits already having been paid). As Calender-Robinson concedes, Tech-Bilt requires only "rough approximations."

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
